tweets.text: @sickjew That is extremely pedantic. He asked in the most inviting possible way, leaving Cosby no excuse. Everyone knew allegations = rape
@sickjew I think a lot of mainstream America would gladly have taken excuse of “ambushed with question” if he had done that, he was careful
@sickjew you can’t ignore how mainstream view Cosby, Simon asked in a way that would make truth transparent to wanna-be disbelievers.
@sickjew I just think we disagree it was a softball. So he might as well not of, if not explicitly?
@sickjew I agree. But by that standard does Simon get no credit for bringing it up in interview? For getting more attention in the issue?
@sickjew I don’t think that was the goal. The goal was getting more attention on rape allegations against Cosby, and that succeeded, no?
RT @sickjew: @mrejfox Journalism isn't about being "overly kind" as a quid pro quo for access. It's about challenging people in power and r…
@sickjew I don’t think kindness was for access- it was for leaving Cosby no possible out for silence. Otherwise he’d claim “attack/ambush”
@sickjew I have seen so many articles about Cosby’s silence regarding the rape allegations based on that interview, seems like more know now
@sickjew Cosby not responding to multiple public allegations of drugging/raping women, despite 3 overly kind attempts, extremely damning
@sickjew that is a perspective I hadn’t considered. I think asking the question at all in an unrelated interview is braver than 99% of media
@sickjew I think if he hadn’t asked in most possible inviting way, Cosby’s silence would not be so damning. He has no excuse with 3 chances.
@sickjew thank you for positive discussion. I agree with you. I disagree with criticizing that particular interview for it.
@sickjew I agree we shouldn’t shy away from explaining the charges elsewhere. I disagree that Simon should have brought it up in question
@sickjew I read the article both times you posted it. If you want to engage me, let’s have a productive discussion. I am not your enemy.
@sickjew Imho a lot of people became aware of the issue after the Hannibal Burress bit went viral, I don’t think it was a hidden issue
@sickjew This has taken a weird turn: you mean the multiple rape allegations against Cosby in the past 10 years and particularly this year?
@sickjew about the “allegations made in recent days” I really dont understand the insistence on pretending we all don’t know what that means
@sickjew Cosby backing down from the politest, calmer, most inviting way to ask that is much more telling than if he had asked explicitly
@sickjew he could have said “no I didn’t do it” or “I am sorry to those involved” and Simon was as understanding and inviting as possible
@sickjew I really don’t get the criticism. All the people involved knew what he was talking about. If he had answered, would have been clear
@sickjew You are arguing with me like I am a troll- we agree on 99% of things. Complaining about that interview is not one of them.
@sickjew I know you are not serious. You, me, Simon, Cosby, everyone else with access to Twitter and the Internet knows what charges.
@sickjew The question would be “Do you have any response to those charges” if we want to continue to be pedantic
@sickjew I’m sorry but do you want to pretend that you, me, Simon, and Cosby all don’t understand what “these allegations” means?
@sickjew Scott Simon phrased the question perfectly to not give Cosby excuse of being “attacked”- he very carefully invited him to talk
@sickjew complaining about HOW someone asks a touchy question is ridiculous - if he didn’t ask, would be complaining about that, but he did
Dumb. I love the way liberals can complain about anything @sickjew: No, NPR Did Not Ask Cosby About Rape Accusations http://t.co/hIvFUPFGQj
